chapter: Chapter: When People Rebel – 1857 and After

questions.question: What was the Revolt of 1857?
questions.answer: The Revolt of 1857 was a major uprising against British rule in India started by Indian soldiers in 1857.

questions.question: Where did the Revolt of 1857 begin?
questions.answer: The Revolt of 1857 began at Meerut in May 1857.

questions.question: Who were sepoys?
questions.answer: Sepoys were Indian soldiers employed in the British East India Company’s army.

questions.question: Why were sepoys unhappy with the British?
questions.answer: Sepoys were unhappy due to discrimination, low pay and the introduction of the greased cartridges.

questions.question: What was the greased cartridge issue?
questions.answer: New rifle cartridges were believed to be greased with cow and pig fat, which offended Hindu and Muslim soldiers.

questions.question: Name two leaders of the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: Two important leaders of the revolt were Rani Lakshmibai and Bahadur Shah Zafar.

questions.question: Who was Bahadur Shah Zafar?
questions.answer: Bahadur Shah Zafar was the last Mughal emperor who was declared the leader of the revolt in Delhi.

questions.question: Who was Rani Lakshmibai?
questions.answer: Rani Lakshmibai was the queen of Jhansi who fought bravely against British forces during the revolt.

questions.question: Name two important centres of the revolt.
questions.answer: Delhi and Kanpur were two important centres of the revolt.

questions.question: Why did the revolt spread to many places?
questions.answer: Many rulers, peasants and soldiers were unhappy with British rule and joined the revolt.

questions.question: How did the British suppress the revolt?
questions.answer: The British used strong military force and recaptured important cities.

questions.question: When did the Revolt of 1857 end?
questions.answer: The revolt was suppressed by the British in 1858.

questions.question: What happened to Bahadur Shah Zafar after the revolt?
questions.answer: Bahadur Shah Zafar was arrested and sent into exile in Rangoon.

questions.question: Why is the Revolt of 1857 important in Indian history?
questions.answer: It was the first major uprising against British rule in India.

questions.question: What change happened after the revolt in 1858?
questions.answer: The rule of the East India Company ended and the British Crown took direct control of India.

questions.question: Who became the ruler of India after 1858?
questions.answer: The British Crown became the ruler of India after 1858.

questions.question: What was the new title given to the Governor-General?
questions.answer: The Governor-General was given the new title of Viceroy.

questions.question: Why did the British reorganise the army after the revolt?
questions.answer: The British reorganised the army to prevent future rebellions.

questions.question: Explain one cause of the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: One major cause was the greased cartridge issue which offended the religious beliefs of soldiers.

questions.question: Explain the role of soldiers in the revolt.
questions.answer: Sepoys started the revolt by rebelling against their British officers.

questions.question: Explain the role of rulers in the revolt.
questions.answer: Several Indian rulers such as Rani Lakshmibai and Nana Sahib joined the revolt against British rule.

questions.question: Explain why the revolt did not succeed.
questions.answer: The revolt lacked unity, strong leadership and modern weapons compared to the British army.

questions.question: Explain the importance of leaders in the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: Leaders such as Rani Lakshmibai inspired people to fight against British rule.

questions.question: Explain the role of Delhi in the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: Delhi became the main centre of the revolt when rebels declared Bahadur Shah Zafar as their leader.

questions.question: Explain why the Revolt of 1857 is called a turning point in Indian history.
questions.answer: After the revolt, the British government took direct control of India and changed its administrative policies.
